---
title: "Software Containers"
subtitle: "Encapsulating computational environments with Docker"
author: "Samuel Pawel, Peter Degen, Fabio Molo, Gorka Fraga González"
date-format: long
preview-links: true
include-in-header:
  - text: |
      <style>
      #title-slide .title {
        font-size: 1.8em;
        color: #29487d;
      }
      </style>
format:
  revealjs:
    menu: true
    pdf: true
    code-line-numbers: false
    smaller: true
    fig-align: left
    logo: "img/uzh_logo_d_pos_web_assoc.jpg"
    embed-resources: true
    progress: true
    slide-number: true
    footer: "Center for Reproducible Science and Research Synthesis | [Home](../../index.qmd)"
    title-slide-attributes:
      data-background-size: contain
      data-background-position: bottom
      data-background-opacity: "0.8"
bibliography: references.bib
nocite: |
  @Nust2020
  @Boettiger2017
  @FragaGonzalez2024
  @Peikert2021
---

## The problem: software environments change

::: incremental
-   Code that runs today may **not run next year**:
    -   `Today`: R 4.5, ggplot2 3.5.2 → `Next year`: R ❓, ggplot2 ❓

-   Different collaborators may have **different software versions** installed

-   "Works on my machine" 😅

-   Journals increasingly require **computational reproducibility**

. . .

**Solution: software containers (e.g., Docker)**
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Containerization with Docker

::::::: columns
::::: {.column width="75%"}
::: incremental
-   **Software containers** encapsulate the full computational environment

-   Computational environment components:

    -   Operating system
    -   R and R packages
    -   Numerical software libraries (e.g., LAPACK, BLAS)
    -   other software (e.g., LaTeX, Java)

-   **Docker** is a popular tool for containerization

-   **Rocker** is a Docker image for R (<https://rocker-project.org/>)
:::

::: fragment
Dockerfile

``` dockerfile
# Uses a Rocker container image with R version 4.4
FROM rocker/verse:4.4

# Install R package versions at time when R 4.4 was up-to-date
RUN install2.r ggplot2 dplyr kableExtra
```
:::
:::::

::: {.column width="25%"}
![[<https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Docker-svgrepo-com.svg>]{style="font-size:0.7em"}](img/docker.png)
:::
:::::::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Containers vs. virtual machines

| | Container | Virtual Machine |
|---|---|---|
| **Isolation** | Process-level | Full OS |
| **Size** | MB | GB |
| **Startup** | Seconds | Minutes |
| **Portability** | High | Moderate |
| **Overhead** | Low | High |

::: fragment
Containers share the host OS kernel — they are **lightweight** and **fast**.
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Key Docker concepts

| Concept | Description |
|---|---|
| **Image** | A read-only template (built from a `Dockerfile`) |
| **Container** | A running instance of an image |
| **Dockerfile** | Text file with instructions to build an image |
| **Layer** | Each instruction in a Dockerfile creates a cached layer |
| **Registry** | Repository of images (e.g., Docker Hub, GitHub Container Registry) |
| **Volume** | Mount a host directory inside a container |

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Building and running a container

``` bash
# Build an image from a Dockerfile in the current directory
docker build -t my-analysis .

# Run a container interactively
docker run --rm -it my-analysis bash

# Run with a mounted project directory
docker run --rm \
  -v "$(pwd)":/home/rstudio/project \
  -p 8787:8787 -e PASSWORD=rstudio \
  my-analysis

# Open RStudio Server at http://localhost:8787
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Rocker: Docker for R

``` dockerfile
# Start from a versioned Rocker image (R + RStudio + LaTeX + Quarto)
FROM rocker/verse:4.4.2

# Install additional R packages pinned via renv or install2.r
RUN install2.r --error \
    ggplot2 \
    dplyr \
    kableExtra \
    renv
```

::: fragment
Rocker images available at <https://rocker-project.org/>:

-   `rocker/r-ver` — R only
-   `rocker/rstudio` — R + RStudio Server
-   `rocker/verse` — + tidyverse + LaTeX + Quarto
-   `rocker/ml` — + machine learning libraries
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Putting it all together

:::::: columns
::: {.column width="55%"}
![Modified from @Peikert2021](img/workflow-quarto.png)
:::

:::: {.column width="45%"}
::: fragment
Terminal

``` {.bash style="font-size:0.85em"}
# clone git repository
git clone git@gitlab.uzh.ch:crsuzh/zurich-names.git

# enter project repository
cd zurich-names

# run automated analyses inside Docker
make docker-report
```

→ `report.html`

[https://gitlab.uzh.ch/crsuzh/zurich-names](https://gitlab.uzh.ch/crsuzh/zurich-names){style="font-size:0.65em"}
:::
::::
::::::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## CRS Primer on Software Containers

:::::: columns
:::: {.column width="45%"}
![<https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.13757230>](img/primer-containers.png)
::::
::::::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Additional Resources

-   Docker documentation: <https://docs.docker.com/get-started/>
-   Rocker project: <https://rocker-project.org/>
-   @Nust2020: The Rockerverse — packages and applications for containerization with R
-   @Boettiger2017: An introduction to Rocker
-   The Turing Way — Reproducible Environments: <https://the-turing-way.netlify.app/reproducible-research/renv>
-   @Peikert2021: combining Git, Make, and Docker in a full reproducible workflow

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## References {.smaller}
